growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron reported record fiscal Q3 results, guided Q4 revenue to $50 billion plus or minus $1 billion, and said HBM4 is already in high-volume shipments, which is the clearest proof point in this batch that AI memory demand is translating into earnings power.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is the cleanest value-plus-growth setup in the batch, with record AI memory results, tight supply visibility, and a still-low forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom and OpenAI unveiled an LLM-optimized intelligence processor on June 24, 2026, with deployment aimed at gigawatt-scale data centers, while Broadcom's latest quarterly release showed AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion, up 143% year over year, confirming monetization at scale beyond the Nvidia trade.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom offers scaled AI semiconductor monetization at a still-reasonable forward multiple for its growth rate.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft has sold off sharply in 2026 after AI-capex concerns and now trades well below its 52-week high, but analysts still see Azure and Copilot as durable growth engines with limited downside at current levels.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft combines mega-cap quality, Azure/Copilot growth, strong FCF, and a near-term earnings catalyst after a major drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The Iran-related conflict cycle and allied missile replenishment are feeding directly into Lockheed's munitions backlog, with a reported $35 billion THAAD contract and record backlog growth that should support continued earnings momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has a credible multi-year defense backlog catalyst, but limited near-term analyst upside keeps it below strong-buy territory.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow is already monetizing enterprise AI at scale, with Q2 subscription revenue up 24.5% to $3.877 billion, current RPO up 21% to $13.2 billion, and AI annual contract value crossing $1 billion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow combines washed-out valuation versus its history with fresh Tier 1 evidence that enterprise AI monetization is real.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Meta has a major binary event on Jul. 29, 2026 with Q2 earnings, and Raymond James raised its price target to $850 on Jul. 21, giving the stock a high-expectation catalyst window.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Meta still offers large-cap growth at a reasonable forward multiple, with Q2 earnings as the immediate test of AI capex discipline.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: EPAM Systems is 61% off its $222.53 high at $85.83, still earns $386.7M annually, and reports Aug. 6 after TD Cowen and Wedbush cut targets into the July earnings window, so sentiment is washed out while the AI and cost-optimization story is still intact; bear case is client delays and macro uncertainty keep recovery slow.
growth_picks.analyst_take: EPAM is a cheap, cash-generative overlap name with a short-squeeze catalyst and an earnings reset ahead.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Alphabet was hit hard by the July 23 AI-spend selloff, but the stock still looks like a profitable franchise trading at a much lower multiple than its growth profile suggests.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet's AI-capex selloff leaves a high-margin franchise trading at a reasonable multiple with strong target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The U.S. Commerce Department said TSMC will add $100 billion more to Arizona investment for a total of $265 billion, deepening the U.S.-Taiwan industrial tie-up just as Beijing considers tighter controls on foreign chipmakers and Chinese use of TSMC services.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TSMC remains the highest-quality AI manufacturing toll road, with Arizona expansion reducing but not eliminating geopolitical risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Quiver's July 22 insider roundup said Elevance Health's CEO and a director bought $1.4M combined, which is the kind of synchronized open-market management buying that tends to matter after a selloff.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Elevance offers a contrarian managed-care entry with cheap forward valuation, strong FCF, and meaningful CEO insider buying after a selloff.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lumentum was upgraded on renewed AI networking demand, laser shortages, and margin expansion, while its NVIDIA partnership and new U.S. manufacturing investments suggest hyperscaler optics demand is still outrunning supply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lumentum is a higher-risk AI optics buy, backed by NVIDIA capital support and strong hyperscaler optical demand.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal is about 45% below its 52-week high and now has a hard catalyst on Jul 28 earnings plus ongoing takeover and turnaround chatter; if the new CEO shows cost discipline and branded checkout stabilizes, the multiple can rerate, but growth is still sluggish.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is an imperfect but compelling value setup with cheap earnings, heavy buybacks, and a near-term earnings/takeover catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: T-Mobile just got punished after Q2 despite beating EPS and raising free-cash-flow guidance, leaving the stock about 25% to 27% below its high; this is a classic post-earnings flush with a buyback backstop, though saturation and ARPU pressure could cap the bounce.
growth_picks.analyst_take: T-Mobile looks like a post-earnings overreaction: FCF guidance improved, the multiple is reasonable, and the selloff reset entry risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Boston Scientific reports Q2 on Jul. 29, 2026, and Truist's Jul. 16 target of $62 implies roughly 42% upside, so the setup combines a near-term earnings catalyst with supportive sell-side expectations.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Boston Scientific is cheap for a high-margin medtech compounder, with a major drawdown and Q2 earnings catalyst, but recall/guidance noise caps conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Northrop's record backlog and higher 2026 guidance are being driven by geopolitical rearmament, especially Sentinel ICBM and other weapons demand tied to the broader Iran-Ukraine conflict backdrop.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop is a cheaper defense-backlog play than RTX, with real order visibility but margin-quality concerns.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's rare-earth magnet exports to the U.S. are still about 20% below pre-trade-war levels, and new EU/U.S. critical-mineral controls keep supply tight, which directly supports the only major U.S. rare-earth producer and magnet supply-chain builder.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is a high-risk strategic minerals buy where policy support and U.S. magnet supply-chain urgency offset weak current profitability.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: The administration's plan to cut aluminum tariffs for companies investing in U.S. smelting directly favors Century Aluminum, which explicitly welcomed the policy and is tied to new domestic smelter investment.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap forward earnings and U.S. aluminum policy support outweigh cyclical and cash-flow risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The widening Middle East shipping crisis is tightening LNG availability for Europe and Asia, and Cheniere's U.S. Gulf Coast export terminals make it one of the clearest beneficiaries of a world that now values secure non-Middle East LNG supply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere remains a high-quality energy-security beneficiary with earnings visibility, though upside is not deep-value.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Red Sea attacks and Bab el-Mandeb disruption are pushing crude tanker rates higher and increasing ton-miles, which should flow directly into Frontline's spot-sensitive earnings and cash generation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Spot tanker exposure gives Frontline direct upside from disrupted crude routes and strong margins.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Q1 2026 13F data shows Price T. Rowe added 15.2M shares, Capital World added 10.3M, and Two Sigma added 9.0M, a broad institutional convergence behind the name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Roblox is a high-risk growth rebound with institutional accumulation, positive FCF, and a near-term earnings catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Quiver's July 8 coverage highlighted Defiance ETFs adding 12.67M shares (~$434M) and BlackRock adding 3.82M shares (~$131M) in Q1 2026, making IREN a crowded institutional AI-infrastructure bet.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IREN has AI-infrastructure optionality and squeeze potential, but the balance sheet and cash burn prevent high conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Credo's AI connectivity ramp is accelerating: current coverage projects about US$600 million of AI optics revenue, the company already said fiscal 2026 revenue more than tripled to $1.3 billion, and its DustPhotonics acquisition expands the 800G to 3.2T optical stack for hyperscale clusters.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Credo's AI-connectivity growth is exceptional, but valuation and beta require a disciplined entry.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Albertsons is down 45% from its $20.91 52-week high to $11.44 after the July 23 earnings shock, but it still generated $217.4M of FY2025 net income and trades at 5.96x forward earnings with share repurchases still in the story; bear case is grocery-margin compression and leverage staying stubbornly high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Albertsons is a leveraged post-earnings value dislocation with strong FCF yield and unusually large target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX is benefiting from the same missile-replenishment cycle, with Raytheon demand for Patriots, AMRAAMs, and Tomahawks pushing backlog to a record level and prompting another full-year guide raise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX fundamentals are improving, but the market has already repriced much of the backlog and missile-cycle benefit.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv is expanding AI-ready cooling manufacturing in Malaysia and Italy and just announced an integrated power and liquid-cooling deployment for NVIDIA's GB300 at the Naval Postgraduate School, confirming that its power and thermal stack is already being pulled by real AI buildouts.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv is a real AI infrastructure winner, but valuation and beta leave little room for disappointment ahead of earnings.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: GE Vernova's Q2 results showed 88% organic order growth, a $176 billion backlog, and more than $5 billion of data-center orders year to date, making its power-generation and electrification businesses a direct beneficiary of AI-driven electricity demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova has excellent AI-power-cycle evidence, but the stock is priced for sustained perfection after a major rerating.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Snowflake's AI Data Cloud strategy is now showing up in product launches and partner traction, with July releases around OpenAI model support, CoCo, CoWork, Horizon Catalog, and a $6 billion AWS collaboration to accelerate enterprise agentic AI adoption.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Snowflake's AI Data Cloud traction is credible, but the stock is too close to highs and too expensive for new money here.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Corning is still roughly 43% below its high after the AI-fiber rally got sold, but Q2 earnings on Jul 28 and the ongoing data-center optical demand story give it a near-term reset point; bear case is that valuation stays rich if guidance softens.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Corning has a legitimate AI fiber angle, but valuation and soft sequential expectations argue for waiting until Q2 results.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anthony Noto has bought shares in the open market five times in 2026, totaling 130,211 shares, and that insider alignment comes just before SoFi's Jul. 29 earnings report.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SoFi has growth and insider alignment, but valuation, beta, and limited target upside make the pre-earnings setup too speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Western Digital is a direct beneficiary of the current memory-and-storage rotation as BofA called the chip selloff a summer reset, not a fundamental reversal, and memory demand tied to AI remains constructive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Western Digital has strong AI storage fundamentals, but after the rerating the remaining upside is not compelling enough for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Intel's Q2 results today beat estimates, revenue rose 25% year over year, and management improved the outlook, which is a fresh turnaround-news setup rather than a stale narrative.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Intel's turnaround is finally showing evidence, but the stock already discounts a lot while FCF and GAAP profitability remain weak.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: USA Rare Earth is a direct beneficiary of the U.S.-China rare-earth decoupling trade because China put it on its export-control list while the company is simultaneously consolidating its mine-to-magnets platform through a $2.8 billion merger.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strategic rare-earth optionality is real, but negative cash flow and extreme execution risk keep it speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Tariffs on imported steel are still being defended as durable policy, and Cleveland-Cliffs says that protection is keeping domestic prices elevated enough to support what it calls its strongest second half in five years.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tariff support could stabilize steel pricing, but weak margins, leverage, and limited target upside block a buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Tractor Supply is 53% off its $63.99 high at $30.32 after the July 23 Q2 print and guidance cut, yet it remains profitable with $1.01B of net income and management is closing Petsense stores, expanding pet services, and reallocating capital, which can turn the miss into a reset; bear case is that the 2026 growth thesis just got weaker and analyst cuts may keep coming.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The drawdown is tempting, but the guidance cut and weak growth make the turnaround unproven.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: UWM Holdings is 74% below its $7.14 high at $1.84, still profitable with $66.36M of net income and a 21.7% dividend yield, and it reports Aug. 5-6 after Morgan Stanley and Barclays both cut targets in July, making this a rate-sensitive capitulation setup; bear case is mortgage origination stays depressed if rates do not cooperate.
growth_picks.analyst_take: UWM is a rate-sensitive capitulation and squeeze setup, but leverage and negative FCF make it too fragile for a buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Viatris has a near-term binary catalyst with a July 30 PDUFA date for its weekly contraceptive patch, and the calendar suggests a meaningful stock move if the FDA decision lands favorably.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Viatris is cheap and cash-generative, but the PDUFA catalyst is already close and target upside is thin.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Red Sea and Hormuz disruptions have pushed refining margins to record levels, and Barron's and WSJ coverage says U.S. refiners are extracting outsized profits from scarce gasoline and diesel supply, with Valero specifically cited as a major beneficiary of the crack-spread surge.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Valero benefits from strong refining margins, but the stock already discounts much of the energy-security tailwind.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: On July 20, 2026, CEO Thomas J. Kemly bought 53,057 shares at $10.00 ($530,570), Sr. EVP/COO Steven M. Klein bought 50,000 shares at $10.00 ($500,000), and director Robert Van Dyk bought 25,000 shares at $10.00 ($250,000), a same-day cluster of open-market bank buying that looks like management conviction after weakness.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The insider cluster is notable, but low institutional ownership and analyst downside keep the bank too thinly supported.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: A July 23, 2026 call sweep bought 1,000 Tesla contracts at the $350 strike expiring August 21, 2026, with 10,091 contracts of day volume and 525 volume/OI, which is fresh bullish positioning ahead of the next catalyst window.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tesla's options-flow signal is overwhelmed by extreme valuation, missed earnings, negative free cash flow, and unclear AI/robotaxi payback.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: President and 10% owner Andrew Kai Kang bought 272,405 shares at $36.71 for $9,999,988 on July 14, 2026, and the filing history shows earlier May accumulation by the same insider, making this a rare eight-figure insider conviction buy.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The insider buy is eye-catching, but poor disclosure, no institutional base, and weak fundamentals make it uninvestable.
